orchestrator_notes: CW-1 (Critical): update_project_mission() and update_agent_mission() do pure replacement — multi-phase projects lose all previous mission context. CW-3 (Medium): report_progress() deletes all AgentTodoItems then re-inserts — continuation sessions lose prior todo history. Both are destructive overwrites with no append/merge mode.

sessions.title: Research & Validate Overwrite Risk
planned_tasks: Read and document current update_project_mission() implementation (project_service.py:549-626)
Read and document current update_agent_mission() implementation (orchestration_service.py:2575-2674)
Read and document current report_progress() todo handling (orchestration_service.py:1278-1506)
Find ALL callers of these 3 functions across the codebase
Check MCP tool schema definitions in mcp_http.py for current parameter signatures
Read thin_prompt_generator.py and protocol_builder.py to understand how agents are instructed to call these tools
Identify exact overwrite scenarios: when does data get silently destroyed?
Check if Project model has any mission history/versioning fields
Check AgentTodoItem model for any soft-delete or archival mechanism
Propose fix approaches for CW-1 (mission append mode) with backward compatibility analysis
Propose fix approaches for CW-3 (todo append mode) with backward compatibility analysis
Assess cascading impact: does changing these signatures break existing agents mid-session?
Write findings to this chain_log.json
tasks_completed: Read update_project_mission() body — confirmed pure .values(mission=mission) replacement
Read update_agent_mission() body — confirmed pure job.mission = mission replacement
Read report_progress() body — confirmed DELETE-all-then-INSERT strategy for AgentTodoItem
Found ALL callers of all 3 functions (MCP, ToolAccessor, REST API, tests)
Checked MCP tool schemas — none have a mode parameter
Read thin_prompt_generator.py — agents instructed to call update_project_mission with full mission
Read protocol_builder.py — agents told to send full todo_items list every call
Checked Project model — NO mission_history or versioning fields exist
Checked AgentJob model — NO mission_history or versioning fields exist
Checked AgentTodoItem model — NO session_id, execution_id, or soft-delete mechanism
Searched codebase for mission_history/previous_mission — zero results
Proposed 3 fix approaches for CW-1 and 3 fix approaches for CW-3
Assessed backward compatibility — all recommended approaches are backward-compatible
FOLLOW-UP: Investigated early termination flow (cancel_project + termination prompt)
FOLLOW-UP: Investigated orchestrator handover/continuation flow (simple_handover + retirement/continuation prompts)
FOLLOW-UP: Determined overwrite behavior is BY DESIGN for all existing flows
deviations: Discovered a 4th caller: REST API PATCH /{job_id}/mission endpoint in operations.py:104-182 also does direct job.mission = request.mission replacement (not via OrchestrationService).

MAJOR DEVIATION: Follow-up investigation of early termination and orchestrator handover flows revealed the overwrite behavior is INTENTIONAL and CORRECT for all existing code paths. See 'follow_up_investigation' section below. Original bug classification is challenged.

cw1_mission_overwrite.severity_reassessment: NOT A BUG — overwrite is intentional design
cw1_mission_overwrite.current_behavior: Both update_project_mission() and update_agent_mission() perform pure replacement of the mission field. ProjectService uses SQLAlchemy .values(mission=mission) UPDATE statement. OrchestrationService uses ORM assignment job.mission = mission. There is no append mode, no history preservation, and no warning. The REST API endpoint in operations.py also does direct job.mission = request.mission assignment.
cw1_mission_overwrite.why_not_a_bug: Continuation orchestrators are EXPLICITLY PROHIBITED from calling update_project_mission(). The continuation prompt (thin_prompt_generator.py:152) states: 'Do NOT re-write the project mission'. The hypothetical 'multi-phase overwrite' scenario described in the original bug report CANNOT HAPPEN in practice because the prompt guardrails prevent it. The mission field stores the CURRENT execution plan (not a log) and is only written during initial staging.

model_details.project_model: src/giljo_mcp/models/projects.py:Project — has 'mission' (Text column, line 93), NO mission_history, NO previous_mission, NO versioning. Has meta_data JSONB but unused for mission history.
model_details.agent_job_model: src/giljo_mcp/models/agent_identity.py:AgentJob — has 'mission' (Text column, line 68), NO history fields. Has job_metadata JSONB but unused for mission history.

existing_callers: REST API PATCH /{job_id}/mission (operations.py:104-182) -> direct ORM (NOT via service layer)

agent_instructions.thin_prompt_generator: Orchestrators told: Step 4 — PERSIST MISSION: update_project_mission(project_id, mission). Continuation orchestrators EXPLICITLY PROHIBITED: 'Do NOT re-write the project mission' (line 152).
agent_instructions.protocol_builder: Step 5: update_project_mission(project_id, mission) as part of staging sequence. Step 7: update_agent_mission(job_id, mission) to persist execution strategy. Both are single-write operations during initial staging only.

risk_scenarios_debunked.original_claim: Multi-phase project: Phase 2 continuation calls update_project_mission — Phase 1 mission is gone
risk_scenarios_debunked.reality: Continuation orchestrators are explicitly told 'Do NOT re-write the project mission' in thin_prompt_generator.py:152. This scenario cannot happen via the standard flow.

risk_scenarios_debunked.original_claim: Mission refinement mid-execution destroys original plan
risk_scenarios_debunked.reality: No existing flow calls update_project_mission after staging. The function is a single-write during staging, not an iterative tool.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach A: Add mode parameter (append/replace)
proposed_fixes.description: Add mode: str = 'replace' parameter to update_project_mission() and update_agent_mission(). mode='replace' preserves current behavior (default). mode='append' reads existing mission, appends new content with separator.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Was recommended before follow-up investigation. Now NOT recommended because the overwrite behavior is correct by design and no existing flow needs append mode.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach B: mission_history JSONB column
proposed_fixes.description: Add mission_history JSONB column for audit trail.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Over-engineered. No existing flow needs mission history.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach C: Separate append_to_mission() tool
proposed_fixes.description: New MCP tool for appending to existing mission.
proposed_fixes.rationale: No existing flow needs this tool.

cw3_todo_overwrite.severity_reassessment: NOT A BUG — replace strategy is intentional and correct
cw3_todo_overwrite.current_behavior: report_progress() uses DELETE-all-for-job then INSERT. Every call sends the COMPLETE todo list with current statuses. The replace strategy ensures the dashboard always shows the authoritative current state.
cw3_todo_overwrite.why_not_a_bug: The replace strategy is correct because: (1) Agents always send the FULL list every call per protocol instructions ('IMMEDIATELY call report_progress() with your updated todo_items list'). (2) During termination, the orchestrator sends the final complete list with items marked 'skipped' — replace is correct. (3) During handover, the old orchestrator finalizes its items in the retirement flow, then the new orchestrator intentionally starts fresh with new items — replace is correct. (4) Append would create DUPLICATE items after each report_progress() call since the full list is sent every time.

report_progress.todo_items_schema: array of {content: string, status: enum[pending, in_progress, completed, skipped]}

agent_instructions.protocol_builder: Agents instructed: 'IMMEDIATELY call report_progress() with your updated todo_items list' — sends FULL list every time. Replace strategy is the correct semantic for full-list-every-time pattern.
agent_instructions.thin_prompt_generator: Continuation orchestrators send fresh todos after retirement flow finalizes old items. Both steps use replace correctly.

cw3_todo_overwrite.rest_api_note: REST API POST /{job_id}/progress passes legacy format without todo_items — does NOT trigger the DELETE.

risk_scenarios_debunked.original_claim: Continuation session sends only its new todos, losing predecessor's items
risk_scenarios_debunked.reality: The handover protocol ensures the OLD orchestrator finalizes its items during retirement (Step 2b + Step 4 of retirement prompt). The NEW orchestrator then intentionally starts fresh. This is the designed sequence, not a bug.
Items transition from old->finalized to new->fresh as part of the handover protocol. The user initiates the handover via the UI button, so this is an expected state change.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach A: Execution-scoped DELETE
proposed_fixes.description: Add execution_id column to scope DELETE to current execution only.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Was recommended before follow-up investigation. Now NOT recommended because: (1) it would BREAK the handover flow — the new orchestrator needs replace behavior to clear stale items, (2) it would cause item accumulation across executions that the dashboard isn't designed to display, (3) the replace strategy is the correct semantic for the full-list-every-time protocol.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach B: Add mode parameter (replace/merge)
proposed_fixes.description: Add mode parameter for merge behavior.
proposed_fixes.rationale: No existing flow needs merge mode. Replace is correct.

proposed_fixes.name: Approach C: Separate add_todo_items() tool
proposed_fixes.rationale: No existing flow needs this tool.

follow_up_investigation.trigger: User requested validation that overwrite behavior doesn't conflict with early termination and orchestrator handover flows

flow_1_early_termination.mechanism: Two-part: (A) ProjectService.cancel_project() sets status='cancelled' — does NOT touch missions or todos. (B) Termination prompt (api/endpoints/prompts.py:625) generates instructions pasted into orchestrator terminal.
flow_1_early_termination.calls_report_progress: YES — orchestrator instructed to call report_progress() per agent with FULL list marking incomplete items as 'skipped'. Replace is the correct semantic (provides final authoritative state).

flow_2_orchestrator_handover.mechanism: UI button triggers POST /api/agent-jobs/{job_id}/simple-handover which returns two prompts: (1) retirement prompt for OLD terminal, (2) continuation prompt for NEW terminal. Same job_id/agent_id reused.
flow_2_orchestrator_handover.calls_update_project_mission: NO — continuation prompt EXPLICITLY PROHIBITS this: 'Do NOT re-write the project mission' (thin_prompt_generator.py:152)
flow_2_orchestrator_handover.calls_update_agent_mission: NO — continuation orchestrator reads mission via get_agent_mission(), never writes
flow_2_orchestrator_handover.calls_report_progress: YES — (1) OLD orchestrator calls report_progress() in retirement to finalize items with proper statuses, (2) NEW orchestrator calls report_progress() with fresh items like [{content: 'Continuation startup', status: 'in_progress'}] — INTENTIONALLY replacing stale items.

conclusion.explanation: The overwrite behavior of all three functions is the INTENDED DESIGN for all existing code paths. update_project_mission() and update_agent_mission() are single-write-during-staging tools — continuation orchestrators are prohibited from calling them. report_progress() uses replace because agents send the FULL todo list every call (not incremental diffs), and the handover protocol explicitly relies on replace to transition from old->finalized to new->fresh items. Changing these to append/merge would BREAK the handover flow and cause item duplication.
conclusion.recommendation_for_orchestrator: CLOSE THIS CHAIN. Neither CW-1 nor CW-3 are bugs — they are correct by design. If the orchestrator wants to proceed anyway as defense-in-depth, the risk is introducing regressions into two working flows (termination + handover) that depend on the current behavior. The original bug report assumed scenarios that cannot happen given the prompt guardrails.

proposed_fix_cw1.summary: Overwrite is intentional. Continuation orchestrators are explicitly prohibited from calling update_project_mission(). The function is a single-write tool used only during staging. No code change recommended.
proposed_fix_cw1.original_recommendation: Was: Add mode='replace'/'append' parameter. Withdrawn after follow-up investigation showed append mode has no use case in any existing flow.

proposed_fix_cw3.summary: Replace strategy is intentional. Agents send the FULL todo list every call. The handover protocol relies on replace to clear stale items. Scoping DELETE by execution_id would BREAK the handover flow. No code change recommended.
proposed_fix_cw3.original_recommendation: Was: Add execution_id to AgentTodoItem. Withdrawn after follow-up investigation showed execution-scoped DELETE would break the handover flow where the new orchestrator intentionally replaces old items.

files_investigated: src/giljo_mcp/thin_prompt_generator.py (continuation prompt: 61+, retirement prompt: 160+, orchestrator instructions)

callers: MCP tool (mcp_http.py:326) -> ToolAccessor -> ProjectService [ONLY during staging]
MCP tool (mcp_http.py:338) -> ToolAccessor -> OrchestrationService [ONLY during staging Step 7]
MCP tool (mcp_http.py:539) -> ToolAccessor -> OrchestrationService [agents during work, termination, handover]
REST API POST /{job_id}/progress (progress.py:25) [legacy format, no todo_items, no DELETE]
callers_found.used_by_termination: YES — orchestrator sends full final list with 'skipped' items (replace is correct)
callers_found.used_by_handover: YES — old orchestrator finalizes, new orchestrator starts fresh (replace is correct)

sessions.notes_for_next: RECOMMENDATION: CLOSE THIS CHAIN WITHOUT IMPLEMENTATION. Both CW-1 and CW-3 are working as designed. The original bug report assumed continuation orchestrators would call update_project_mission() (they are explicitly prohibited) and that report_progress() replace behavior loses data (the handover protocol ensures proper sequencing of old->finalize then new->fresh). Implementing the proposed fixes would risk breaking the handover and termination flows that depend on the current overwrite behavior. If the orchestrator still wants defense-in-depth, the ONLY safe enhancement would be logging/audit (emit a log line when mission is overwritten or todos are replaced) — but this is observability, not a bug fix.
sessions.summary: FOLLOW-UP INVESTIGATION OVERTURNS ORIGINAL FINDINGS. Both CW-1 and CW-3 are NOT BUGS — the overwrite behavior is intentional and correct for all existing code paths. CW-1: Continuation orchestrators are explicitly prohibited from calling update_project_mission() ('Do NOT re-write the project mission' — thin_prompt_generator.py:152). The function is a single-write tool used only during initial staging. CW-3: report_progress() replace strategy is correct because agents send the FULL todo list every call, and the handover protocol explicitly relies on replace to transition items between old and new orchestrators. Implementing append/merge/execution-scoping would BREAK the handover flow. Recommendation: CLOSE chain 0766 without implementation.

Implement chosen fix for CW-3 (todo overwrite protection)

Update chain_log.json with results
sessions.notes_for_next: 0766a research concluded both CW-1 and CW-3 are NOT BUGS. Overwrite behavior is by design. Implementation session should be CANCELLED unless orchestrator overrides this recommendation.

chain_summary: Research-only chain. Follow-up investigation of early termination and orchestrator handover flows revealed that the overwrite behavior reported as bugs (CW-1, CW-3) is actually intentional design. Continuation orchestrators are prohibited from calling update_project_mission(), and report_progress() replace strategy is relied upon by the handover protocol. Recommendation: CLOSE without implementation.
